translation:
Śrī Śuka continued:
Seated comfortably on a couch and received with high respect by Balarāma and Kṛṣṇa, he (Akrūra) realized all the aspirations he cherished on his way to Gokula.

devanagari: किमलभ्यं भगवति प्रसन्ने श्रीनिकेतने । तथापि तत्परा राजन् न हि वाञ्छन्ति किञ्चन ॥ २ ॥

translation: Is there anything unattainable to a person when the glorious Lord, the very abode of goddess Śrī, becomes propitious? However, those who are exclusively devoted to him expect nothing from him, Oh King!

devanagari: सायंतनाशनं कृत्वा भगवान् देवकीसुतः । सुहृत्सु वृत्तं कंसस्य पप्रच्छान्यच्चिकीर्षितम् ॥ ३ ॥

translation: When the supper was over, Lord Kṛṣṇa, the son of Devakī, enquired about the treatment meted out by Kaṃsa to his Kinsmen and his further plans in the matter.

devanagari: श्रीभगवानुवाच । तात सौम्यागतः कच्चित् स्वागतं भद्रमस्तु वः । अपि स्वज्ञातिबन्धूनां अनमीवमनामयम् ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: śrībhagavānuvāca tāta saumyāgataḥ kaccit svāgataṃ bhadramastu vaḥ api svajñātibandhūnāṃ anamīvamanāmayam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
The glorious Lord enquired:[1]
Oh gentle-natured uncle! It is good that you have come. I hope you had a comfortable journey. May be well with you all. Are all our (near and distant) kinsmen[2] and friends un-harassed (by Kaṃsa) and free from ailments?
purport:
Footnote [1]:
The following four verses (4,5,6 and 7) are not found in Padaratnāvalī’s text.

Footnote [2]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā explains: jñāti—near relatives. They are sapiṇḍa; bāndhavas are distant and hence a-sapiṇḍa.

devanagari: किं नु नः कुशलं पृच्छे एधमाने कुलामये । कंसे मातुलनाम्न्यङ्ग स्वानां नस्तत्प्रजासु च ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: kiṃ nu naḥ kuśalaṃ pṛcche edhamāne kulāmaye kaṃse mātulanāmnyaṅga svānāṃ nastatprajāsu ca
translation: Dear uncle! It is certainly not very consistent on my part to enquire after the welfare of our kinsmen and that of their descendants (or of the subjects in general), so long as this co-called maternal uncle Kaṃsa, the very bane (lit. disease) of the family, is prospering.

devanagari: अहो अस्मदभूद् भूरि पित्रोर्वृजिनमार्ययोः । यद्धेतोः पुत्रमरणं यद्धेतोर्बन्धनं तयोः ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: aho asmadabhūd bhūri pitrorvṛjinamāryayoḥ yaddhetoḥ putramaraṇaṃ yaddhetorbandhanaṃ tayoḥ

translation: Alas! It is for my sake that my venerable parents, despite their innocence, had to undergo immense sufferings. It is for me that their sons were murdered and they had to remain in chains in the prison.

devanagari: दिष्ट्याद्य दर्शनं स्वानां मह्यं वः सौम्य काङ्क्षितम् । सञ्जातं वर्ण्यतां तात तवागमनकारणम् ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: diṣṭyādya darśanaṃ svānāṃ mahyaṃ vaḥ saumya kāṅkṣitam sañjātaṃ varṇyatāṃ tāta tavāgamanakāraṇam

translation: It is our good fortune that today we could have a sight of our relative—a sight I had been coveting for long. Oh gentle-natured uncle! Be pleased to explain to us the purpose of your visit. Oh uncle.

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । पृष्टो भगवता सर्वं वर्णयामास माधवः । वैरानुबन्धं यदुषु वसुदेववधोद्यमम् ॥ ८ ॥

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
When thus enquired by Lord Kṛṣṇa, Akrūra, the descendant of Madhu, described in details the history and rationale of Kaṃsa’s deep-rooted hostility to the Yadu clan, his attempts on the life of Vasudeva.

devanagari: यत्सन्देशो यदर्थं वा दूतः सम्प्रेषितः स्वयम् । यदुक्तं नारदेनास्य स्वजन्मानकदुन्दुभेः ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: yatsandeśo yadarthaṃ vā dūtaḥ sampreṣitaḥ svayam yaduktaṃ nāradenāsya svajanmānakadundubheḥ

translation: He reported to Kṛṣṇa the message (inviting them on the pretext of dhanur-yāga and the proposed attempt on their life through Cāṇūra and others) and the mission for which he had been deputed as an emissary by Kaṃsa and what Nārada had narrated to Kaṃsa about his (Kṛṣṇa’s) birth from Vasudeva.

devanagari: श्रुत्वाक्रूरवचः कृष्णो बलश्च परवीरहा । प्रहस्य नन्दं पितरं राज्ञा दिष्टं विजज्ञतुः ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: śrutvākrūravacaḥ kṛṣṇo balaśca paravīrahā prahasya nandaṃ pitaraṃ rājñā diṣṭaṃ vijajñatuḥ
translation: On hearing the message from Akrūra, Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma, the vanquishers of hostile warriors, laughed loudly, and they communicated to their foster-father Nanda the command of the king Kaṃsa.

devanagari: गोपान् समादिशत् सोऽपि गृह्यतां सर्वगोरसः । उपायनानि गृह्णीध्वं युज्यन्तां शकटानि च ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: gopān samādiśat so upāyanāni gṛhṇīdhvaṃ yujyantāṃ śakaṭāni ca
translation: He (Nanda) on his part ordered the cowherds.: “Let all the yield of cows (such as milk, ghee) as well as presents (of various kinds) as tribute, be taken with you. Let the bullock-carts be kept ready.

devanagari: यास्यामः श्वो मधुपुरीं दास्यामो नृपते रसान् । द्रक्ष्यामः सुमहत्पर्व यान्ति जानपदाः किल । एवमाघोषयत्क्षत्रा नन्दगोपः स्वगोकुले ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: yāsyāmaḥ śvo madhupurīṃ dāsyāmo nṛpate rasān drakṣyāmaḥ sumahatparva yānti jānapadāḥ kila evamāghoṣayatkṣatrā nandagopaḥ svagokule
translation: Tomorrow we have to go to Mathurā where we shall be paying as tribute the choicest products of our cows’ yield to the king. We shall also witness there a very grand festival for attending which (it is reported that) all the people from the country (of Śūrasena) are going.” Nanda, the Gopa chief, got this order proclaimed all over his Vraja, through the security officer of the Vraja.

devanagari: गोप्यस्तास्तद् उपश्रुत्य बभूवुर्व्यथिता भृशम् । रामकृष्णौ पुरीं नेतुं अक्रूरं व्रजमागतम् ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: gopyastāstad upaśrutya babhūvurvyathitā bhṛśam rāmakṛṣṇau purīṃ netuṃ akrūraṃ vrajamāgatam

translation: The cowherd women (to whom Kṛṣṇa was their very life) were deeply distressed at heart on hearing that Akrūra had come to Vraja to take away Balarāma and Kṛṣṇa to the capital city (Mathurā).

devanagari: काश्चित् तत्कृतहृत्ताप श्वासम्लानमुखश्रियः । स्रंसद्दुद्दुकूलवलय केशग्रंथ्यश्च काश्चन ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: kāścit tatkṛtahṛttāpa śvāsamlānamukhaśriyaḥ sraṃsadduddukūlavalaya keśagraṃthyaśca kāścana

translation: Some had the splendour of their countenance withered by the hot breaths due to the agony of heart caused by hearing that news. Some became so much beside themselves through overwhelming grief that they got their garments loosened, bangles dropping and braids of hair dishevelled.

devanagari: अन्याश्च तदनुध्यान निवृत्ताशेषवृत्तयः । नाभ्यजानन् इमं लोकं आत्मलोकं गता इव ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: anyāśca tadanudhyāna nivṛttāśeṣavṛttayaḥ nābhyajānan imaṃ lokaṃ ātmalokaṃ gatā iva
translation: Through their concentrated contemplation on Kṛṣṇa, some Gopīs got all the functions of their sense-organs ceased and became insensible to this external world, as if they had ascended to the realm of the Supreme Self.

devanagari: स्मरन्त्यश्चापराः शौरेः अनुरागस्मितेरिताः । हृदिस्पृशश्चित्रपदा गिरः संमुमुहुः स्त्रियः ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: smarantyaścāparāḥ śaureḥ anurāgasmiteritāḥ hṛdispṛśaścitrapadā giraḥ saṃmumuhuḥ striyaḥ

translation: Some other Gopīs lost their consciousness as they remembered the wonderfully phrased speeches of Kṛṣṇa touching their very heart and expressed with warm loving smiles.

devanagari: गतिं सुललितां चेष्टां स्निग्धहासावलोकनम् । शोकापहानि नर्माणि प्रोद्दामचरितानि च ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: gatiṃ sulalitāṃ ceṣṭāṃ snigdhahāsāvalokanam śokāpahāni narmāṇi proddāmacaritāni ca

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [vocative single from √ snih class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ snih class 4 verb]
[vocative single from √ snih class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ snih class 4 verb]

translation: Musing over Kṛṣṇa’s extremely graceful gait and manner of behaviour, his amiable smiles and loving glances, his witty repartee dispelling sorrows and his extra-ordinary exploits, the Gopīs terrified and distressed at the very thought of impending separation, met together in groups and with tears flowing over their cheeks (faces) and hearts absorbed in Kṛṣṇa, they complained:

devanagari: चिन्तयन्त्यो मुकुन्दस्य भीता विरहकातराः । समेताः सङ्घशः प्रोचुः अश्रुमुख्योऽच्युताशयाः ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: cintayantyo mukundasya bhītā virahakātarāḥ sametāḥ saṅghaśaḥ procuḥ aśrumukhyo
translation: Musing over Kṛṣṇa’s extremely graceful gait and manner of behaviour, his amiable smiles and loving glances, his witty repartee dispelling sorrows and his extra-ordinary exploits, the Gopīs terrified and distressed at the very thought of impending separation, met together in groups and with tears flowing over their cheeks (faces) and hearts absorbed in Kṛṣṇa, they complained:

devanagari: श्रीगोप्य ऊचुः । अहो विधातस्तव न क्वचिद् दया । संयोज्य मैत्र्या प्रणयेन देहिनः । तांश्चाकृतार्थान् वियुनङ्क्ष्यपार्थकं । विक्रीडितं ते...
english_devnagari: śrīgopya ūcuḥ aho vidhātastava na kvacid dayā saṃyojya maitryā praṇayena dehinaḥ tāṃścākṛtārthān viyunaṅkṣyapārthakaṃ vikrīḍitaṃ te

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ ceṣṭ ], [accusative single from √ ceṣṭ ]

translation:
Gopīs said:
Alas! Oh Creator! You have not the slightest compassion anywhere in you, inasmuch as, you bring together embodied beings in friendliness and love and unnecessarily separate them before they have accomplished their desired object. Your whimsical behaviour is childish (lit. like the purposeless behaviour of a child).

devanagari: यस्त्वं प्रदर्श्यासितकुन्तलावृतं । मुकुन्दवक्त्रं सुकपोलमुन्नसम् । शोकापनोदस्मितलेशसुन्दरं । करोषि पारोक्ष्यमसाधु ते कृतम् ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: yastvaṃ pradarśyāsitakuntalāvṛtaṃ mukundavaktraṃ sukapolamunnasam śokāpanodasmitaleśasundaraṃ karoṣi pārokṣyamasādhu te kṛtam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

[nominative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

translation: It is not a good act (in fact a wicked) act on your part that you, having first manifested to us the countenance of Kṛṣṇa covered over with ringlets of dark hair, charming due to its beautiful cheeks and prominent nose, beautiful with a beaming smile that dispelled all grief, now takes it away from our view.

devanagari: क्रूरस्त्वमक्रूरसमाख्यया स्म नः । चक्षुर्हि दत्तं हरसे बताज्ञवत् । येनैकदेशेऽखिलसर्गसौष्ठवं । त्वदीयमद्राक्ष्म वयं मधुद्विषः ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: krūrastvamakrūrasamākhyayā sma naḥ cakṣurhi dattaṃ harase batājñavat yenaikadeśe tvadīyamadrākṣma vayaṃ madhudviṣaḥ
translation: You are really cruel as (disguising yourself) under the nomenclature Akrūra[3], you like an irrational person, are depriving us of our very eye given by you to us. It is with that we visualized the excellence of your creative capacity (completely exhibited) even in a part (limb) of Kṛṣṇa, the slayer of Madhu.
purport:
Footnote [3]:
You will say it is Akrūra and not I, who is taking away Kṛṣṇa. It is not possible for others to do this. It is you who have come here under the name Akrūra—Bhāvāratha Dīpikā

devanagari: न नन्दसूनुः क्षणभङ्गसौहृदः । समीक्षते नः स्वकृतातुरा बत । विहाय गेहान् स्वजनान् तान् पतीन् । तद्दास्यमद्धोपगता नवप्रियः ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: na nandasūnuḥ kṣaṇabhaṅgasauhṛdaḥ samīkṣate naḥ svakṛtāturā bata vihāya gehān svajanān tān patīn taddāsyamaddhopagatā navapriyaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ das ], [accusative single from √ das ]

translation: Alas! What a pity! The friendship of Nanda’s son is of a momentary nature. He is so much fond of new company that he does not condescend to cast a glance at us who have been eagerly longing for him and who, being beguiled by his enchanting acts, have openly accepted his slavery forsaking our homes, relatives, sons and husbands.

devanagari: सुखं प्रभाता रजनीयमाशिषः । सत्या बभूवुः पुरयोषितां ध्रुवम् । याः संप्रविष्टस्य मुखं व्रजस्पतेः । पास्यन्त्यपाङ्गोत् कलितस्मितासवम् ॥ २...
english_devnagari: sukhaṃ prabhātā rajanīyamāśiṣaḥ satyā babhūvuḥ purayoṣitāṃ dhruvam yāḥ saṃpraviṣṭasya mukhaṃ vrajaspateḥ pāsyantyapāṅgot kalitasmitāsavam

translation: For the ladies of the city (Mathurā), their night will dawn very happily. All the benedictions have been (i.e. will be) definitely realized by them. For as Kṛṣṇa enters the city, they will drink (with their eyes) the countenance of the Lord of Vraja (Kṛṣṇa) full of intoxicating (yet) nectar-like smiles, the charm of which is enhanced by lovely side-glances.

devanagari: तासां मुकुन्दो मधुमञ्जुभाषितैरः । गृहीतचित्तः परवान् मनस्व्यपि । कथं पुनर्नः प्रतियास्यतेऽबला । ग्राम्याः सलज्जस्मितविभ्रमैर्भ्रमन् ॥ ...
english_devnagari: tāsāṃ mukundo madhumañjubhāṣitairaḥ gṛhītacittaḥ paravān manasvyapi kathaṃ punarnaḥ pratiyāsyate grāmyāḥ salajjasmitavibhramairbhraman
translation: Oh ladies! Even if Mukunda (Kṛṣṇa) is self-controlled and independent and has his relatives in Vraja, how can he ever return to us, poor rustic women, when his mind is captivated by the sweet, soft words, and fascinated by the bashful smiles and amorous deportment of the ladies of Mathurā.

devanagari: अद्य ध्रुवं तत्र दृशो भविष्यते । दाशार्हभोजान्धक वृष्णिसात्वताम् । महोत्सवः श्रीरमणं गुणास्पदं । द्रक्ष्यन्ति ये चाध्वनि देवकीसुतम् ॥ ...
english_devnagari: adya dhruvaṃ tatra dṛśo bhaviṣyate dāśārhabhojāndhaka vṛṣṇisātvatām mahotsavaḥ śrīramaṇaṃ guṇāspadaṃ drakṣyanti ye cādhvani devakīsutam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ dṛś class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ dṛś class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ dṛś class 1 verb]

[nominative single from √ sū class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ sū class 6 verb]
[nominative single from √ sū class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ sū class 2 verb]

translation: Today, certainly there will be a high feast to the eyes of Dāśārhas, Bhojas, Andhakas, Vṛṣṇis and Sātvatas and those who will see passing by the road (in the chariot) Kṛṣṇa, the son of Devakī, the consort of goddess Śrī (Lakṣmī), the very abode of excellences.

devanagari: मैतद्विधस्याकरुणस्य नाम भूद् । अक्रूर इत्येतदतीव दारुणः । योऽसावनाश्वास्य सुदुःखितं जनं । प्रियात्प्रियं नेष्यति पारमध्वनः ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: maitadvidhasyākaruṇasya nāma bhūd akrūra ityetadatīva dāruṇaḥ yo priyātpriyaṃ neṣyati pāramadhvanaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative dual from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative dual from √ i class 2 verb], [locative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[nominative dual from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative dual from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative dual from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: As this fellow wants to carry off Kṛṣṇa who is dearer than life itself to us, beyond the known paths (to a far-off land beyond our reach), without (in any way) consoling deeply-grieving persons (like us), Akrūra (not cruel) ought not to be the name of such a ruthless fellow who is (as a matter of fact) extremely cruel.

devanagari: अनार्द्रधीरेष समास्थितो रथं । तमन्वमी च त्वरयन्ति दुर्मदाः । गोपा अनोभिः स्थविरैरुपेक्षितं । दैवं च नोऽद्य प्रतिकूलमीहते ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: anārdradhīreṣa samāsthito rathaṃ tamanvamī ca tvarayanti durmadāḥ gopā anobhiḥ sthavirairupekṣitaṃ daivaṃ ca no

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ tvar ], [vocative plural from √ tvar ], [accusative plural from √ tvar ]

translation: Look! without feeling the slightest feeling (lit. witness) of sympathy or mercy he (Kṛṣṇa) has comfortably occupied his seat in the chariot; following him the haughty Gopas (cowherds) are hastily speeding up in their bullock carts. (This departure of Kṛṣṇa) is treated with indifference by the elders (of Vraja). It appears that hostile Destiny is working against us today.

devanagari: निवारयामः समुपेत्य माधवं । किं नोऽकरिष्यन् कुलवृद्धबान्धवाः । मुकुन्दसङ्गान्निमिषार्धदुस्त्यजाद् । दैवेन विध्वंसितदीनचेतसाम् ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: nivārayāmaḥ samupetya mādhavaṃ kiṃ no mukundasaṅgānnimiṣārdhadustyajād daivena vidhvaṃsitadīnacetasām
translation: Approaching Kṛṣṇa (the Lord of Lakṣmī) We shall dissuade him (from going). What can the elders of the family and relatives do unto us whose hearts have been rendered afflicted and melancholy by Fate, by bringing about separation from Mukunda (Kṛṣṇa) which is unbearable even for half a minute, (under such circumstances even death is no terror to us.)

devanagari: यस्यानुरागललितस्मितवल्गुमन्त्र । लीलावलोकपरिरम्भणरासगोष्ठाम् । नीताः स्म नः क्षणमिव क्षणदा विना तं । गोप्यः कथं न्वतितरेम तमो दुरन्तम्...
english_devnagari: yasyānurāgalalitasmitavalgumantra līlāvalokaparirambhaṇarāsagoṣṭhām nītāḥ sma naḥ kṣaṇamiva kṣaṇadā vinā taṃ gopyaḥ kathaṃ nvatitarema tamo durantam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [vocative single from √ lal class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ lal ]
[vocative single from √ lal class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ lal ]
[nominative plural from √ nī class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ nī class 1 verb]
[nominative plural from √ nī class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ nī class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ nī class 1 verb]

translation: Oh cowherd women! How can we get over without him the unending darkness of separation from him, with whom we felt like a moment the nights indulged in rāsa dance (and similar other sports) the charm of which was enhanced by his warm love, charming smiles, sweet whispers, playful glances and embraces?

devanagari: योऽह्नः क्षये व्रजमनन्तसखः परीतो । गोपैर्विशन् खुररजश्छुरितालकस्रक् । वेणुं क्वणन् स्मितकटाक्षनिरीक्षणेन । चित्तं क्षिणोत्यमुमृते नु क...
english_devnagari: yo gopairviśan khurarajaśchuritālakasrak veṇuṃ kvaṇan smitakaṭākṣanirīkṣaṇena cittaṃ kṣiṇotyamumṛte nu kathaṃ bhavema

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ viś class 6 verb], [vocative single from √ viś class 6 verb]
[nominative single from √ kvaṇ class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ kvaṇ class 1 verb]

translation: How can we (even manage to) live without Kṛṣṇa who at the even tide, with Balarāma (the incarnation of Śeṣa) as his compassion, and surrounded by cowherds, used to enter Vraja, playing on his flute, and with his curly hair and wreaths of flowers soiled heavily with dust raised by the hoofs of the cattle, and captivate our hearts by casting at us his smiling side-glances?

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । एवं ब्रुवाणा विरहातुरा भृशं । व्रजस्त्रियः कृष्णविषक्तमानसाः । विसृज्य लज्जां रुरुदुः स्म सुस्वरं । गोविन्द दामोदर माधव...
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca evaṃ bruvāṇā virahāturā bhṛśaṃ vrajastriyaḥ kṛṣṇaviṣaktamānasāḥ visṛjya lajjāṃ ruruduḥ sma susvaraṃ govinda dāmodara mādhaveti

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Talking to each other in this manner, and extremely distressed at the thought of separation from Kṛṣṇa, the women of Vraja whose hearts were deeply attached to Kṛṣṇa threw to winds their sense of bashfulness and cried loudly, (uttering his various names:) “Oh Govinda! Alas Dāmodara! Oh Mādhava”.

devanagari: स्त्रीणामेवं रुदन्तीनां उदिते सवितर्यथ । अक्रूरश्चोदयामास कृतमैत्रादिको रथम् ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: strīṇāmevaṃ rudantīnāṃ udite savitaryatha akrūraścodayāmāsa kṛtamaitrādiko ratham

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ vad class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ vad class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ vad class 1 verb], [locative single from √ vad class 1 verb]
[nominative dual from √ vad class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ vad class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ vad class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ vad class 1 verb]

translation: [4] When it was sun-rise, Akrūra finished his morning Sandhyā (prayer to the sun) and other religious duties, and drove his chariot (along with Śrī Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma) without paying any heed to the lamentations of womenfolk.
purport:
Footnote [4]:
A Bhāvāratha Dīpikā gives an additional verse here:
krūram akrūrataḥ śrutvā kaṃsaṃ kaṃsaniṣūdanaḥ /
gopī-manorathārāmaḥ sa-rāmaḥ svapuraṃ yayau //
Having heard from Akrūra (the report about) cruel Kaṃsa, Kṛṣṇa, the slayer of Kaṃsa, the delight of the Gopīs (by fulfilling their desires) went to his capital (Mathurā) along with Balarāma.

devanagari: गोपास्तमन्वसज्जन्त नन्दाद्याः शकटैस्ततः । आदायोपायनं भूरि कुम्भान् गोरससम्भृतान् ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: gopāstamanvasajjanta nandādyāḥ śakaṭaistataḥ ādāyopāyanaṃ bhūri kumbhān gorasasambhṛtān
translation: Cowherds, headed by Nanda, followed them closely in their bullock-carts taking with them abundant tribute in the form of pitchers full of the yield of cows (such as milk, curds, ghee).

devanagari: गोप्यश्च दयितं कृष्णं अनुव्रज्यानुरञ्जिताः । प्रत्यादेशं भगवतः काङ्क्षन्त्यश्चावतस्थिरे ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: gopyaśca dayitaṃ kṛṣṇaṃ anuvrajyānurañjitāḥ pratyādeśaṃ bhagavataḥ kāṅkṣantyaścāvatasthire

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ añj ], [vocative plural from √ añj ]
[nominative plural from √ añj ], [vocative plural from √ añj ], [accusative plural from √ añj ]
[nominative plural from √ kāṅkṣ class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ kāṅkṣ class 1 verb]

translation: The cowherd women also followed their beloved Kṛṣṇa feeling a bit delighted when he turned round and cast an affectionate glance at them and waited[5] for his command not to follow him.
purport:
Footnote [5]:
Waited for his reply to the message sent by them through an emissary and stood on an eminence within the range of his view—VT.

devanagari: तास्तथा तप्यतीर्वीक्ष्य स्वप्रस्थाणे यदूत्तमः । सान्त्वयामस सप्रेमैः आयास्य इति दौत्यकैः ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: tāstathā tapyatīrvīkṣya svaprasthāṇe yadūttamaḥ sāntvayāmasa sapremaiḥ āyāsya iti dautyakaiḥ
translation: Perceiving that the Gopīs were deeply tormented at his departure, Kṛṣṇa, the Lord of Yadus, comforted them through a messenger that he would return soon.[6]
purport:
Footnote [6]:
VT. strongly objects to the interpretation: “I shall be sending you messages and thus see you through messengers though personally I cannot come”, for this contradicts the words of Kṛṣṇa’s special emissary Uddhava:
āgamiṣyatyadīrgheṇa kālena vrajam atyutaḥ /

devanagari: यावदालक्ष्यते केतुः यावद्रेणू रथस्य च । अनुप्रस्थापितात्मानो लेख्यानीवोपलक्षिताः ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: yāvadālakṣyate ketuḥ yāvadreṇū rathasya ca anuprasthāpitātmāno lekhyānīvopalakṣitāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ man class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ man class 8 verb]

[nominative plural from √ likh class 6 verb], [vocative plural from √ likh class 6 verb], [accusative plural from √ likh class 6 verb], [nominative plural from √ likh ], [vocative plural from √ likh ], [accusative plural from √ likh ]

translation: As long as the flag (of the chariot) was visible and as long as the dust raised by the chariot could be seen, they stood motionless like figures painted in pictures, as they had deputed their minds after him.

devanagari: ता निराशा निववृतुः गोविन्दविनिवर्तने । विशोका अहनी निन्युः गायन्त्यः प्रियचेष्टितम् ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: tā nirāśā nivavṛtuḥ govindavinivartane viśokā ahanī ninyuḥ gāyantyaḥ priyaceṣṭitam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ ceṣṭ ], [accusative single from √ ceṣṭ ]

translation: At long last, being disappointed at the return of Lord Kṛṣṇa, they too returned and for relieving themselves of the sorrow of separation, they spent days (and nights) in glorifying his deeds and pastimes in songs.

devanagari: भगवानपि सम्प्राप्तो रामाक्रूरयुतो नृप । रथेन वायुवेगेन कालिन्दीं अघनाशिनीम् ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: bhagavānapi samprāpto rāmākrūrayuto nṛpa rathena vāyuvegena kālindīṃ aghanāśinīm

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ yu class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ yu class 6 verb], [nominative single from √ yu class 9 verb]

translation: In his chariot swift in speed like the wind, the Lord along with Balarāma and Akrūra arrived (at noon) on the bank of the Yamunā that absolves one of all sins.

devanagari: इति श्रीमद्‍भागवते महापुराणे पारमहंस्यां । संहितायां दशमस्कन्धे पूर्वार्धे अक्रूरप्रतियाने एकोन्चत्वारिंशोऽध्यायः ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: iti śrīmad‍bhāgavate mahāpurāṇe pāramahaṃsyāṃ saṃhitāyāṃ daśamaskandhe pūrvārdhe akrūrapratiyāne ekoncatvāriṃśo
translation: After (washing his hands and feet) performing the Acamana (ritual sipping of water), he drank the sweet crystalclear water of the Yamunā. Going near a grove of trees (where the chariot was standing), he got into it along with Balarāma.

devanagari: तत्रोपस्पृश्य पानीयं पीत्वा मृष्टं मणिप्रभम् । वृक्षषण्डमुपव्रज्य सरामो रथमाविशत् । अक्रूरस्तावुपामन्त्र्य निवेश्य च रथोपरि । कालिन्द्...
english_devnagari: tatropaspṛśya pānīyaṃ pītvā mṛṣṭaṃ maṇiprabham vṛkṣaṣaṇḍamupavrajya sarāmo rathamāviśat akrūrastāvupāmantrya niveśya ca rathopari kālindyā hradamāgatya snānaṃ vidhivadācarat

[nominative single from √ mṛś class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ mṛś class 6 verb]

translation: Akrūra on his part seated them (comfortably) in the chariot, and (requesting them not to leave their seats as he was afraid of enemies) he took leave of them. Coming to the pool of Yamunā (known as ‘Ananta tīrtha’), he performed ablution according to śāstric injunctions.

devanagari: निमज्ज्य तस्मिन् सलिले जपन् ब्रह्म सनातनम् । तावेव ददृशेऽक्रूरो रामकृष्णौ समन्वितौ ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: nimajjya tasmin salile japan brahma sanātanam tāveva dadṛśe

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ jap class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ jap class 1 verb]

translation: As he plunged into the river water reciting the ancient Vedic mantras (of Agha-marṣaṇa), that Akrūra (being of purified mind) saw therein both Balarāma and Kṛṣṇa together.

devanagari: तौ रथस्थौ कथमिह सुतौ आनकदुन्दुभेः । तर्हि स्वित् स्यन्दने न स्त इत्युन्मज्ज्य व्यचष्ट सः ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: tau rathasthau kathamiha sutau ānakadundubheḥ tarhi svit syandane na sta ityunmajjya vyacaṣṭa saḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ su class 5 verb], [vocative dual from √ su class 5 verb], [accusative dual from √ su class 5 verb]
[nominative dual from √ sū class 6 verb], [vocative dual from √ sū class 6 verb], [accusative dual from √ sū class 6 verb]
[nominative dual from √ sū class 2 verb], [vocative dual from √ sū class 2 verb], [accusative dual from √ sū class 2 verb]

translation: ‘How is it that the two sons of Vasudeva who were seated in the chariot are down here? In that case they must not be in the chariot.’ With this suspicion, he emerged out of water and looked for them.

devanagari: तत्रापि च यथापूर्वं आसीनौ पुनरेव सः । न्यमज्जद् दर्शनं यन्मे मृषा किं सलिले तयोः ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: tatrāpi ca yathāpūrvaṃ āsīnau punareva saḥ nyamajjad darśanaṃ yanme mṛṣā kiṃ salile tayoḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: Perceiving both of them seated (in the chariot) as before, he took, a plunge again thinking, “Was my vision of them both in the water unreal?”

devanagari: भूयस्तत्रापि सोऽद्राक्षीत् स्तूयमानमहीश्वरम् । सिद्धचारणगन्धर्वैः असुरैर्नतकन्धरैः ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: bhūyastatrāpi so siddhacāraṇagandharvaiḥ asurairnatakandharaiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ stu class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ stu class 2 verb]

translation: Again he saw in that very water, Śeṣa, the Lord of Serpents—a resplendent God with one thousand heads, with crowns adorning his one thousand hoods, wearing a blue garment, white as lotus-fibers, (enormous) like the white snowclad mountain Kailāsa with its (golden) peaks. He was being praised by Siddhas, Gāraṇas and Gandharvās as well as by Asuras, with their heads bowed down in salutation.

devanagari: सहस्रशिरसं देवं सहस्रफणमौलिनम् । नीलाम्बरं विसश्वेतं शृङ्गैः श्वेतमिव स्थितम् ॥ ४५ ॥
english_devnagari: sahasraśirasaṃ devaṃ sahasraphaṇamaulinam nīlāmbaraṃ visaśvetaṃ śṛṅgaiḥ śvetamiva sthitam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ sthā class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ sthā class 1 verb]

translation: Again he saw in that very water, Śeṣa, the Lord of Serpents—a resplendent God with one thousand heads, with crowns adorning his one thousand hoods, wearing a blue garment, white as lotus-fibers, (enormous) like the white snowclad mountain Kailāsa with its (golden) peaks. He was being praised by Siddhas, Gāraṇas and Gandharvās as well as by Asuras, with their heads bowed down in salutation.

devanagari: तस्योत्सङ्गे घनश्यामं पीतकौशेयवाससम् । पुरुषं चतुर्भुजं शान्तं पद्मपत्रारुणेक्षणम् ॥ ४६ ॥
english_devnagari: tasyotsaṅge ghanaśyāmaṃ pītakauśeyavāsasam puruṣaṃ caturbhujaṃ śāntaṃ padmapatrāruṇekṣaṇam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ śam class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ śam class 9 verb]
[nominative single from √ śam class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ śam class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ śam class 9 verb], [accusative single from √ śam class 9 verb]

translation: He saw on Śeṣa’s lap (coils) another (Supreme) Person (Lord Viṣṇu) bluish dark like a cloud in complexion, clad in yellow silken garment, with four arms, eyes reddish like lotus-petals and very serene and gentle in appearance.

devanagari: चारुप्रसन्नवदनं चारुहासनिरीक्षणम् । सुभ्रून्नसं चरुकर्णं सुकपोलारुणाधरम् ॥ ४७ ॥
english_devnagari: cāruprasannavadanaṃ cāruhāsanirīkṣaṇam subhrūnnasaṃ carukarṇaṃ sukapolāruṇādharam
translation: He had a beautiful and gracious countenance with enchanting smiles and glances, well-shaped eyebrows, prominent nose, beautiful ears, charming cheeks and reddish lips.

devanagari: प्रलम्बपीवरभुजं तुङ्गांसोरःस्थलश्रियम् । कम्बुकण्ठं निम्ननाभिं वलिमत्पल्लवोदरम् ॥ ४८ ॥
english_devnagari: pralambapīvarabhujaṃ tuṅgāṃsoraḥsthalaśriyam kambukaṇṭhaṃ nimnanābhiṃ valimatpallavodaram
translation: He had long and mighty arms, high shoulders and broad chest—the home of the goddess Śrī, a conch-shaped neck, a deep navel and stomach shaped like a tender leaf with three folds.

devanagari: बृहत्कतिततश्रोणि करभोरुद्वयान्वितम् । चारुजानुयुगं चारु जङ्घायुगलसंयुतम् ॥ ४९ ॥
english_devnagari: bṛhatkatitataśroṇi karabhorudvayānvitam cārujānuyugaṃ cāru jaṅghāyugalasaṃyutam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ bṛh ], [vocative single from √ bṛh ], [accusative single from √ bṛh ]
[nominative single from √ bṛh ], [vocative single from √ bṛh ], [accusative single from √ bṛh ]

translation: He had well-formed loins and hips, pair of well- developed thighs like those of an elephant and a pair of beautiful knees and shapely shanks.

devanagari: तुङ्गगुल्फारुणनख व्रातदीधितिभिर्वृतम् । नवाङ्गुल्यङ्गुष्ठदलैः विलसत् पादपङ्कजम् ॥ ५० ॥
english_devnagari: tuṅgagulphāruṇanakha vrātadīdhitibhirvṛtam navāṅgulyaṅguṣṭhadalaiḥ vilasat pādapaṅkajam

[nominative single from √ vṛ class 5 verb], [accusative single from √ vṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ vṛ class 9 verb], [accusative single from √ vṛ class 9 verb]

translation: His ankles were prominent and were surrounded by the halo of light radiating from his reddish toe-nails. His lotusfeet were specially charming with petal-like tender toes and (a pair of) big toes.

devanagari: सुमहार्हमणिव्रात किरीटकटकाङ्गदैः । कटिसूत्रब्रह्मसूत्र हारनूपुरकुण्डलैः ॥ ५१ ॥
english_devnagari: sumahārhamaṇivrāta kirīṭakaṭakāṅgadaiḥ kaṭisūtrabrahmasūtra hāranūpurakuṇḍalaiḥ
translation: He was shining brilliantly with a diadem, bracelets, armlets decked with the most precious jewels as well as with a girdle, a sacred thread, necklace, anklets and ear-rings. He held a lotus, conch, discus and a mace in each of his hands. He had the Śrī vatsa mark on his chest, and a resplendent Kaustubha gem was shining at his neck. He wore a garland of special sylvain flowers (called Vanamālā).

devanagari: भ्राजमानं पद्मकरं शङ्खचक्रगदाधरम् । श्रीवत्सवक्षसं भ्राजत् कौस्तुभं वनमालिनम् ॥ ५२ ॥
english_devnagari: bhrājamānaṃ padmakaraṃ śaṅkhacakragadādharam śrīvatsavakṣasaṃ bhrājat kaustubhaṃ vanamālinam
translation: He was shining brilliantly with a diadem, bracelets, armlets decked with the most precious jewels as well as with a girdle, a sacred thread, necklace, anklets and ear-rings. He held a lotus, conch, discus and a mace in each of his hands. He had the Śrī vatsa mark on his chest, and a resplendent Kaustubha gem was shining at his neck. He wore a garland of special sylvain flowers (called Vanamālā).

devanagari: सुनन्दनन्दप्रमुखैः पर्षदैः सनकादिभिः । सुरेशैर्ब्रह्मरुद्राद्यैः नवभिश्च द्विजोत्तमैः ॥ ५३ ॥
english_devnagari: sunandanandapramukhaiḥ parṣadaiḥ sanakādibhiḥ sureśairbrahmarudrādyaiḥ navabhiśca dvijottamaiḥ
translation: He was being praised in hymns with distinct words expressive of different sentiments, by his attendants, the prominent among whom were Sunanda and Nanda, by sages like Sanaka (and his three brothers Sanandana, Sanātana, Sanatkumāra), by the rulers of gods like god Brahmā, Rudra and others, by nine foremost of Brāhmaṇas (such as Marīci, Kāśyapa and others) and by the most prominent of the Lord’s pure-hearted devotees like Prahlāda, Nārada and Uparicara Vasu.

devanagari: प्रह्रादनारदवसु प्रमुखैर्भागवतोत्तमैः । स्तूयमानं पृथग्भावैः वचोभिरमलात्मभिः ॥ ५४ ॥
english_devnagari: prahrādanāradavasu pramukhairbhāgavatottamaiḥ stūyamānaṃ pṛthagbhāvaiḥ vacobhiramalātmabhiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ stu class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ stu class 2 verb]

translation: He was being praised in hymns with distinct words expressive of different sentiments, by his attendants, the prominent among whom were Sunanda and Nanda, by sages like Sanaka (and his three brothers Sanandana, Sanātana, Sanatkumāra), by the rulers of gods like god Brahmā, Rudra and others, by nine foremost of Brāhmaṇas (such as Marīci, Kāśyapa and others) and by the most prominent of the Lord’s pure-hearted devotees like Prahlāda, Nārada and Uparicara Vasu.

devanagari: श्रिया पुष्ट्या गिरा कान्त्या कीर्त्या तुष्ट्येलयोर्जया । विद्ययाविद्यया शक्त्या मायया च निषेवितम् ॥ ५५ ॥
english_devnagari: śriyā puṣṭyā girā kāntyā kīrtyā tuṣṭyelayorjayā vidyayāvidyayā śaktyā māyayā ca niṣevitam
translation: He was waited upon personally by the deities like Śrī (presiding deity over affluence), Puṣṭi (goddess of nourishment), Sarasvatī (presiding over speech), Kānti (goddess of beautiful complexion and splendour), Kīrti (presiding deity of fame), Tuṣṭi (goddess of contentment), Ilā (the Earth goddess), Ūrjā (goddess of energy and strength), Vidyā (goddess presiding over spiritual enlightenment), Avidyā (the goddess of Nescience), by Śakti (Power) and Māyā (the deluding potency of the Lord).

devanagari: विलोक्य सुभृशं प्रीतो भक्त्या परमया युतः । हृष्यत्तनूरुहो भाव परिक्लिन्नात्मलोचनः ॥ ५६ ॥
english_devnagari: vilokya subhṛśaṃ prīto bhaktyā paramayā yutaḥ hṛṣyattanūruho bhāva pariklinnātmalocanaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ prī class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ prī class 9 verb]
[nominative single from √ yu class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ yu class 6 verb], [nominative single from √ yu class 9 verb]
[nominative single from √ hṛṣ class 4 verb], [vocative single from √ hṛṣ class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ hṛṣ class 4 verb]

translation: He was extremely delighted to have the vision of that Lord and overwhelmed with intense devotion and thrilled with joy (with his hair standing on their ends) his eyes wet with tears of love, Akrūra of the Sāttvata clan summoned courage, and bowed down with his head. And folding his pair of palms, with concentrated attention, he slowly began to praise in voice choked with emotions.

devanagari: गिरा गद्‍गदयास्तौषीत् सत्त्वमालम्ब्य सात्वतः । प्रणम्य मूर्ध्नावहितः कृताञ्जलिपुटः शनैः ॥ ५७ ॥
english_devnagari: girā gad‍gadayāstauṣīt sattvamālambya sātvataḥ praṇamya mūrdhnāvahitaḥ kṛtāñjalipuṭaḥ śanaiḥ
translation:
He was extremely delighted to have the vision of that Lord and overwhelmed with intense devotion and thrilled with joy (with his hair standing on their ends) his eyes wet with tears of love, Akrūra of the Sāttvata clan summoned courage, and bowed down with his head. And folding his pair of palms, with concentrated attention, he slowly began to praise in voice choked with emotions.
